notes:
nữa means “more” or “another” — you use it when you want an extra amount of something.

It usually comes after the noun or at the end of the sentence.  

It can also mean “again” with verbs, showing repetition.  

In negative sentences, it means “anymore” or “no longer.”  

Think of it as “add one more” or “continue again,” depending on position.
exampleSentences: Em uống một ly nước nữa.
Anh mua một cái áo nữa.

definitions.vi: Được sử dụng để chỉ một lượng bổ sung hoặc sự tiếp tục.
definitions.en: Used to indicate an additional amount or continuation.
definitions.es: Se utiliza para indicar una cantidad adicional o continuación.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I drink one more glass of water.
notes_md:
**nữa** means “more” or “another” — you use it when you want an extra amount of something.

It usually comes **after** the noun or at the **end of the sentence**.  

It can also mean “again” with verbs, showing repetition.  

In negative sentences, it means “anymore” or “no longer.”  

Think of it as “add one more” or “continue again,” depending on position.

translation.en: I want to eat this dish again.

sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + uống (drink) + một (one) + ly (glass) + nước (water) + nữa (more)
Anh (he) + mua (buy) + một (one) + cái (classifier) + áo (shirt) + nữa (more)
